content.problemStatement:
You are in a grid of dimensions N×MN \times MN×M.

You are allowed to perform two types of operations:


Go down, left, up, or right each for a cost of XXX. Formally, if you are at the cell (i,j)(i,j)(i,j) of the grid, you can go to either of the cells (i+1,j)(i + 1, j)(i+1,j), (i,j−1)(i, j - 1)(i,j−1), (i−1,j)(i - 1, j)(i−1,j) or (i,j+1)(i, j + 1)(i,j+1) at a cost of XXX.
Go diagonally down-left, down-right, up-left, up-right, for a cost of YYY. Formally, if you are at the cell (i,j)(i,j)(i,j) of the grid, you can go to either of the cells (i+1,j−1)(i + 1, j - 1)(i+1,j−1), (i+1,j+1)(i + 1, j + 1)(i+1,j+1), (i−1,j−1)(i - 1, j - 1)(i−1,j−1) or (i−1,j+1)(i - 1, j + 1)(i−1,j+1) at a cost of YYY.
You cannot exit the grid at any point. Find the minimum cost of going to the bottom-right corner (N,M)(N, M)(N,M) from the top-left corner (1,1)(1, 1)(1,1).

Note:\textbf{Note}:Note: Since input-output is large, prefer using fast input-output methods.


Input Format

First line will contain TTT, number of testcases. Then the testcases follow.
Each testcase contains of a single line of input, four space separated integers integers: N,M,X,YN, M, X, YN,M,X,Y.


Output Format
For each testcase, output the answer in a new line.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 111:
We can go 444 steps down from (1,1)(1, 1)(1,1) to (5,1)(5, 1)(5,1) and then 555 steps right to (5,6)(5, 6)(5,6). The total cost is 2×9=182 \times 9 = 182×9=18
Test Case 222:
We can go 333 steps diagonally down-right from (1,1)(1, 1)(1,1) to (4,4)(4, 4)(4,4) and then 333 steps right to (4,7)(4, 7)(4,7). The total cost is 3×6+3×5=18+15=333 \times 6 + 3 \times 5  = 18 + 15 = 333×6+3×5=18+15=33
Test Case 333:
We can go 666 steps diagonally down-right from (1,1)(1, 1)(1,1) to (7,7)(7, 7)(7,7) and then 111 step right to (7,8)(7, 8)(7,8). The total cost is 5×6+6=365 \times 6 + 6 = 365×6+6=36
